note: This claim is deductive/philosophical, not numerical. 'Inputs' are the predicates, premises, and mereological axioms that ground the derivation. No physical constants are involved; the Speed-of-Causality Principle does not apply here.

predicates.Ind: Ind(x): x is indivisible — has no proper parts.
predicates.Part: Part(y, x): y is a proper part of x.
predicates.Prop_intrinsic: Prop(P, x): P is an intrinsic (non-relational) property of x.
predicates.Reduce: Reduce(P, x): P reduces to the proper parts of x.
predicates.Brute: Brute(P, x): P is a brute primitive of x — no structural reduction.
predicates.Physical: Physical(P): P is a physical property (requires geometric/algebraic structure).
predicates.Rel: Rel(P, x): P is a relational property of x.

premises.source: GCM Tenet T1 — one fundamental particle.

premises.statement: forall x. Ind(x) -> not (exists y. Part(y, x))
premises.source: Definition of 'indivisible' (classical mereology, Leonard & Goodman 1940; Simons 1987).

premises.statement: forall x, P. Prop_intrinsic(P, x) -> (Reduce(P, x) or Brute(P, x))
premises.reading: Any intrinsic (non-relational) property either reduces to an entity's parts or is a brute primitive.
premises.source: Property-grounding principle; standard in analytic metaphysics.

premises.statement: forall x, P. Reduce(P, x) -> (exists y. Part(y, x))
premises.reading: Reduction to parts requires parts to reduce to.

premises.statement: forall P. Brute(P, x) -> not Physical(P)
premises.reading: A brute primitive lacks the geometric/algebraic structure required of a physical property (spin, chirality, charge).
premises.source: Philosophy-of-physics convention; the most contestable premise (see caveats in indivisibility-formal.md).

mereological_axioms.statement: x < y -> exists z. (z <= y) and not Overlap(z, x)
mereological_axioms.name: Weak Supplementation
Atom axiom (instantiated at g)
mereological_axioms.source: GCM T1 via Axiom of Indivisibility.

target_theorem.statement: Ind(g) -> forall P. (not Prop_intrinsic(P, g)) or Rel(P, g)
target_theorem.reading: Every property an indivisible entity possesses must be relational; no intrinsic physical property can be attributed to it.
target_theorem.verification_method: Propositional instantiation at (g, P), then test unsatisfiability of the reductio conjunction.

tools_available_in_session.lean4: installed at ~/.elan/bin/lean (stub archived; not fully verified)
